{{Quote|It's what we've dreamed of, all our lives, especially since the Empire got here... to help somehow... to be the ones who make a difference in what happens...|Barney|<ref name="annual" />}}
'''Barney''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] native of [[Belderone/Legends|Belderone]] who served in the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and later the [[Alliance of Free Planets]]. Barney and his lifelong friend [[Flint]] worked in an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] factory until a team of Rebel agents arrived on Belderone in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. Although Barney and Flint attempted to aid the Rebels, an Imperial [[Battle of Belderone (Galactic Civil War)|reprisal]] destroyed their home village, and caused a despondent Flint to leave to train in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] under [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Barney later trained under [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], and in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] helped foil an Imperial [[Subjugation of Naldar|invasion]] of Naldar led by his old friend Flint.

==Biography==
{{Quote|You couldn't save her or be a hero... so now you're gonna punish the whole universe, and kill a whole lot of mothers and sons and innocent people? That makes a ''lot'' of sense! Frankly, I don't think you can. But I've been wrong before. So, prove it to me, for old time's sake. See the face of one of your victims. Kill me first, Flint.|Barney, to Flint on Naldar|<ref name="marvel92" />}}
A [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], Barney grew up on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Belderone/Legends|Belderone]],<ref name="annual" /> an [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> that served as a major [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] manufacturing site during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Barney and his best friend, [[Flint]], came of age in a town fully controlled by the Empire, working in the local Imperial foundry, although they were kept unaware of what they spent their working days actually helping to build. In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], as the war with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] was reaching its height, the conflict came to Belderone when a [[Starship/Legends|ship]] containing Rebel agents—among them major Rebel leaders [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]—arrived on the world in order to help foment Rebel activity on Belderone and neighboring [[Kulthis]].<ref name="annual" />

[[File:Barney.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Barney in 4 ABY]]
The spectacle of non-Imperial visitors drew Barney and Flint's undivided attention, and the two friends volunteered to be the Rebels' welcoming party—they were especially drawn to Skywalker's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], as Flint's [[Flint Torul|late father]] had been a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]]. Barney and Flint were present in [[Zana|Flint's mother's]] [[Zana's tavern|tavern]] when an Imperial [[Assassin/Legends|assassination]] squad tried and failed to [[Death/Legends|kill]] the Rebels, inspiring them to follow them and attempt to join their cause. The two friends guided the Rebels to the factory where they worked, but arrived to find a fleet of fully armed and operational AT-ATs on the move—the Empire was making a [[Battle of Belderone (Galactic Civil War)|pre-emptive strike]] on Belderone, and were headed directly in the path of Barney and Flint's home village. Barney and Flint suffered a serious [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] crash while racing home, and Barney was knocked unconscious. When he came to he found his hometown destroyed, his entire family dead, and Flint gone, having left to train as a [[Dark side adept/Legends|Dark Side Adept]] under the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. With nothing left for him on his homeworld, Barney left with the Rebels, hoping to fulfill his dream of helping free [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and becoming a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] Knight.<ref name="annual" />

Barney remained with the Rebellion through their [[Battle of Endor/Legends|climactic victory]] at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], and afterwards served the fledgling [[Alliance of Free Planets]] on their Endor base. Barney trained as a soldier directly under Skywalker, but was frustrated by the Jedi's reticence to train him and his other students in [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Barney joined Skywalker and an Alliance detachment to the planet [[Naldar]], not far from his homeworld of Belderone, when the planet's [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Vila]] came to Endor to plead for aid after Imperial forces [[Subjugation of Naldar|subjugated]] the world. On Naldar, Barney discovered to his shock that it was Flint—now an adept of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]—leading the Imperials on Naldar. Skywalker and Flint did battle, but the Jedi was wounded by a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper's]] [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], prompting Barney to step in and reason with his lifelong friend. As Flint proclaimed his plans to lead his Imperial army against the rest of the Alliance in the [[Sector/Legends|sector]], Barney challenged Flint to kill him first. Barney's act of defiance prompted Flint to stand down and dismantle his war machines, ending the Imperial threat in the region.<ref name="marvel92" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|This is just what I've dreamed of... what I've wanted all my life... except this time it's no game... I'm going to get my chance... join the Rebellion... maybe find someone who can really train me as a Jedi Knight...|Barney|<ref name="annual" />}}
Growing up under the thumb of the Galactic Empire and compelled to work as a part of the Imperial war machine, Barney wished above all else to one day be a part of the solution to the galaxy's problems, and long held Rebel sympathies. Barney and his best friend Flint were inseparable in their younger years, and while Barney was occasionally exasperated by Flint's excitable nature, the two considered themselves partners in looking after their community.<ref name="annual" /> Barney knew Flint so well that he was unafraid to confront his old friend even after seeing him at the head of an Imperial army, correctly betting that Flint did not have the cruelty in his heart to kill him.<ref name="marvel92" /> Barney was awed upon witnessing Luke Skywalker use the Force for the first time, and immediately became fascinated with the idea of becoming a Jedi Knight himself—it was the possibility of receiving Jedi training that was a motivating factor for Barney leaving Belderone and joining the Rebellion himself.<ref name="annual" /> However, he later found himself frustrated by Skywalker's reluctance to train him as a Jedi.<ref name="marvel92" />

==Behind the scenes==
Barney first appeared [[Star Wars Annual (1977) 3|''Star Wars Annual'' (1977) 3]], written by [[Mary Jo Duffy]] and released in [[1983]].<ref name="annual" /> He later appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 92|''Star Wars'' (1977) 92]], which serves as a direct sequel of sorts to the previous ''Annual''.<ref name="marvel92" /> In his two comic appearances, Barney was illustrated by [[Klaus Janson]]<ref name="annual" /> and [[Jan Duursema]].<ref name="marvel92" />
